_comment: Encoded-state value -> meaning map (docs/02 §8). Tekken 8's entity struct does not carry the
per-flag booleans PlayerFrame wants (block_stun, hit_stun, airborne, ...). It carries a handful
of ENCODED STATE WORDS whose integer values each denote a whole situation. Outer key = the
player field, inner key = the raw integer as a string, value = the semantic flags it implies.
The decoder reads every mapped field and UNIONS the flags, so overlapping axes compose.
CALIBRATED on exe build 5.02.01 by live observation (docs/02 §8 protocol), 2026-07-11. The word
OFFSETS are facts/data re-sourced from Irony (tomislav-ivankovic/Irony) after the fork's seeded
offsets went stale on this build (stun_type 0x61c, simple_move_state 0x670, complex_move_state
0x68c); the VALUE meanings below were derived only by watching the game, never copied from any
community enum (docs/02 §5 rule 2).
stun_type (0x61c) is the block/hit-outcome axis: 1=blocked, 12=grounded hit, 3/4=hit while
airborne (a juggle). simple_move_state (0x670) is the situation axis: 3=neutral, 6=crouch,
1=attacking, 12=knocked-down/thrown, 25=airborne. complex_move_state (0x68c) is a coarse phase,
carried for raw_state visibility but currently mapped to nothing.
KNOWN GAPS (deliberately unmapped, revisit later): sidestep aliases jump at simple_move_state=25
(the two differ only in state_flags 0x434 bits, which this value->flag schema cannot read);
stagger (incl. wall-stagger) has no distinct value observed yet; wakeup is read as knockdown ->
neutral; counter_state and throw states are not yet located (throw shows via move_id). Unmapped
values contribute no flags and ride out on PlayerFrame.raw_state so the gaps stay visible.
Known flags (offsets.STATE_FLAGS): neutral, attack, recovery, block_stun, hit_stun, stagger,
throw_active, throw_tech, thrown, airborne, juggle, knockdown, wakeup, sidestep, crouch.
An unknown flag name is rejected at load time, not silently dropped at 60fps.
notes: Calibrated on 5.02.01 by observation (docs/02 §8). Offsets re-sourced from Irony after the fork's went stale. See _comment for the axes and the known gaps (sidestep/stagger/wakeup/counter/throw).
